# UC-11.7.4 · Asterisk Codec Mismatch and Transcoding Load from Full Log

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Performance &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch this Asterisk system’s calls and health, so trunks, queues, and voicemail stay in shape for everyday use.*

---

## Description

Codec negotiation failures force software transcoding, raising CPU and latency on virtual PBX nodes.

## Value

Avoids peak-hour brownouts and improves voice quality by aligning allow-lists.

## Implementation

(1) Filter verbose logs to manage volume. (2) Trend by host and trunk. (3) Remediate endpoint and gateway codec lists.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip (sourcetype="asterisk:full" OR sourcetype="asterisk:messages") earliest=-24h
| eval host=coalesce(host, hostname, "")
| where match(lower(_raw), "(?i)transcod|codec.*(mismatch|incompatible)|no joint capabilities|slin32|slin16")
| stats count by host
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Top hosts bar; timechart event rate.

## Known False Positives

Transcoding on mixed endpoints and meeting bridges where transcoding is expected by design.

## References

- [Asterisk Wiki](https://wiki.asterisk.org/)
- [Splunk — Monitor network ports](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/Monitornetworkports)
